extra.message: User-controlled 'id' parameter is concatenated directly into SQL query passed to cursor.execute(). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL commands by manipulating the 'id' query parameter, leading to data exfiltration, modification, or authentication bypass.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'username' and 'password' parameters are concatenated directly into the authentication SQL query. The password parameter is not sanitized (only username gets regex filtering). An attacker can perform SQL injection to bypass authentication entirely.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: The URL path (self.path[1:]) is passed directly to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True. An attacker can send a crafted HTTP method request with an arbitrary command in the path to achieve remote OS command execution.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'domain' parameter is concatenated with a command string and passed to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True. An attacker can inject arbitrary OS commands via semicolons, pipes, or other shell metacharacters in the domain parameter.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'object' parameter is base64-decoded and deserialized via pickle.loads(). Pickle deserialization of untrusted data allows arbitrary code execution because pickled objects can execute arbitrary Python code during the unpickling process via __reduce__.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: The URL path is directly interpolated into a file path string and opened in binary read mode. An attacker can use '../' sequences in the URL to read arbitrary files outside the static directory, including /etc/passwd and application source code.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: The URL path is directly interpolated into a file path string and opened in text read mode. An attacker can use '../' sequences in the URL to read arbitrary files outside the static directory.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' parameter is passed to os.path.abspath() and then used to open a file. os.path.abspath() does not prevent directory traversal. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the server by providing paths like '../../../etc/passwd'.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-controlled 'msg' query parameter is returned directly as HTML content without any HTML escaping. The result is embedded in the page template via {content}, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript (reflected XSS).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Database content retrieved via SQL injection is rendered inside <td> tags without HTML escaping. Since the SQL injection vulnerability allows arbitrary data to be returned, an attacker can inject malicious HTML/JavaScript that executes when the page is viewed (stored XSS).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-submitted comments retrieved from the database are rendered inside <td> tags without HTML escaping. An attacker can submit a malicious comment containing HTML/JavaScript that executes when other users view the guestbook page (stored XSS).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' parameter is injected directly into a JavaScript string inside window.location.replace(). An attacker can break out of the string context with a single quote and inject arbitrary JavaScript, leading to DOM-based XSS and open redirect.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' parameter is injected directly into a JavaScript string inside window.location. An attacker can break out of the string context with a single quote and inject arbitrary JavaScript, leading to DOM-based XSS and open redirect.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' parameter is used directly in window.location.replace() without validation. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external domains, enabling phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' parameter is used directly in window.location without validation. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external domains, enabling phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User-controlled 'path' parameter is passed to urllib.request.urlopen() when it contains '://'. An attacker can use file://, http://, or other URL schemes to make the server issue requests to internal resources (SSRF), including reading local files via file:// URLs or probing internal services.

cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

extra.message: User-controlled 'session' parameter is directly set as the user's SESSIONID cookie value without validation. An attacker can fixate a known session ID, then trick the victim into using it, allowing session hijacking after the victim authenticates.

cwe: CWE-384: Session Fixation

extra.message: The /admin route returns sensitive management links to all users regardless of authentication status. A client-side JavaScript redirect is used instead of a server-side 302 redirect, meaning the sensitive content is served first before the redirect executes. Unauthenticated users can access admin functionality.

cwe: CWE-862: Missing Authorization

extra.message: The email update action on /settings accepts GET requests and modifies the user's email address without any anti-CSRF token validation. An attacker can craft a malicious link or use /

cwe: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

extra.message: The account deletion action on /danger accepts GET requests and deletes user accounts without any anti-CSRF token validation. An attacker can craft a malicious link or use /

cwe: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

extra.message: Session tokens are generated using random.sample() which uses the Mersenne Twister PRNG (not cryptographically secure). An attacker who observes multiple session tokens can predict future tokens and hijack sessions. Use secrets.token_hex() instead.

cwe: CWE-338: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)

extra.message: Hardcoded credentials ('dsvpwa' / 'dsvpwa') are checked in the authentication logic. These credentials bypass the database lookup entirely, providing a backdoor into the application.

cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

extra.message: Session cookies are set without the HttpOnly flag in the cookie attributes. Only 'path' is set on the morsel before OutputString(). This allows client-side JavaScript to access the session cookie, making it vulnerable to theft via XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag

extra.message: Session cookies are set without the Secure flag. When SSL/TLS is enabled, the session cookie will still be transmitted over unencrypted HTTP connections, allowing session hijacking via network sniffing.

cwe: CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute

extra.message: Content-Security-Policy header is set to "default-src * 'unsafe-inline'" which allows scripts from any source and permits inline script execution. This effectively neutralizes the XSS protection that CSP is designed to provide.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: X-XSS-Protection header is explicitly set to '0', disabling the browser's built-in reflected XSS filter. This makes users more vulnerable to reflected XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: The OP_NO_SSLv3 flag is cleared from the SSL context options, enabling the insecure SSLv3 protocol which is vulnerable to the POODLE attack. SSLv3 should be explicitly disabled.

cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength

extra.message: Exception messages are returned directly in the HTTP response body via str(ex). This can leak sensitive information such as internal paths, SQL query structures, or stack traces to the client.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: User passwords are stored in plaintext in the XML configuration file (users.xml). Passwords include 'Y3RFgwZP$v6v#D', 'guest', 'babygirl', and '123456'. No hashing or salting is applied before storage.

cwe: CWE-312: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information

extra.message: Full HTTP request details including request line, status code, and size are logged to stdout. This can expose sensitive data like session tokens in query strings to log files.

cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

extra.message: The 'username' parameter is sanitized with a regex removing non-word characters, but the 'password' parameter is NOT sanitized at all before being used in SQL concatenation. This asymmetry means SQL injection is still achievable through the unsanitized password field.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: No X-Frame-Options header is set in HTTP responses. This allows the application to be rendered in an iframe, making it vulnerable to clickjacking attacks where an attacker can trick users into performing unintended actions.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames
